title: career-ops.steering-queue.requested v1
description: Emitted by career-ops's steering-ask.mjs (career-ops/data/steering-queue.json's write path) every time a new async human-decision entry is queued — by the scanner, the brainstormer framework, or any future caller. Distinct from career-ops.steering.answered.v1, which is an unrelated behavioural-memory-steering concept (target geography/salary/etc, feeding auto-memory). This channel exists so Eric learns of a pending career-ops decision via a real notification instead of manually opening data/steering-queue.json.

id.description: steering-queue.json entry id (8-char hex), used as the notification's correlation/dedup key.

options.description: Suggested answer options, or null for free-text.

urgency.description: Mirrors steering-queue.json's urgency field; high maps to a critical-priority push.

kind.description: Optional typed-ask discriminator (2026-07-18 ask-rail). 'secret_request' asks Eric for a credential: the phone UI stores the typed value straight into hearth-vault (POST /api/vault/credential) and the recorded ANSWER is only the vault pointer — the secret value never rides this bus, the queue file, or kb.

secret_key.description: hearth-vault key name (namespace/key, e.g. api/foo-key) the answer should be stored under. Required by convention when kind=secret_request.
